LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CORNELL CORRECTIONS OF CALIFORNIA, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IF PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE, IN GOOD FAITH, THAT THE CORPORATE DEFENDANT ACTED THROUGH ITS EMPLOYEES. AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ASSAULT AND BATTERY, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT RESTS ON THE SAME GENERAL SET OF FACTS, INVOLVES THE SAME INJURY, AND REFERS TO THE SAME INCIDENT AS THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT. (POINTE SAN DIEGO RES. COMM'Y L.P. V. PROCOPIO, CORY, HARGREAVES, & SAVITCH, LLP (2011) 195 CAL.APP.4TH 265, 276-77, "IN APPLYING THE RELATION-BACK ANALYSIS, COURTS SHOULD CONSIDER THE STRONG POLICY IN THIS STATE THAT CASES SHOULD BE DECIDED ON THEIR MERITS.") Application / Request For Prove-Up Hearing Or Default Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-26-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Andre Doniel Armstrong.
Dept. 514. Judge: Cynthia Ming-Mei Lee. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR #11216. Telephone: 415-509-7557.

Plaintiff Robert Klure was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Andre Doniel Armstrong.
Plaintiff's first amended complaint for negligence, assault & battery was filed on January 19, 2013.
Default waw entered against defendant Andre Doniel Armstrong on June 2, 2015.
Dismissal without prejudice as to defendant Cornell Corrections of California, Inc. was filed on October 16, 2017.
Dismissal without prejudice as to defendant the GEO Group, Inc. was filed on November 1, 2017.
DOE defendants were dismissed on November 1, 2017.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Robert Klure, and against the Defaulted Defendant Andre Doniel Armstrong, as follows:

Damages - $89,959.90.

Total - $89,959.90.

Additionally, any and all proceeds recouped by this judgment shall be paid over to the City and County of San Francisco for satisfaction of the medical lien (dated 11/7/12). Satisfaction of the lien shall discharge Robert Klure from the financial obligation to the City and County of San Francisco for medical expenses from this litigation.

Proposed judment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-05-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Crystal Van Der Putten, Esq. of Livingston Law Firm, Tel. 925.952.9880, for defendant and moving party;
Joshua H. Watson, Esq. of the Dolan Law Firm, Tel. 415.421.2800, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court modified its tentative ruling as the the assault and battery. DEFENDANT CORNELL CORRECTIONS OF CALIFORNIA, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. As to the cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend if Plaintiff can allege, in good faith, that the corporate Defendant acted through its employees. As to the cause of action for assault and battery, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. The First Amended Complaint rests on the same general set of facts, involves the same injury, and refers to the same incident as the original complaint. (Pointe San Diego Res. Comm'y L.P. v. Procopio, Cory, Hargreaves, & Savitch, LLP (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 265, 276-77, "in applying the relation-back analysis, courts should consider the strong policy in this state that cases should be decided on their merits.") Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
